channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: What to watch as Pete Hegseth faces questions about his experience and personal past
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth will publicly face senators for the first time Tuesday after weeks of privately pushing back on criticism over his qualifications and personal past.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Carrie Underwood, Village People to perform at Trump's inauguration events
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Country music star Carrie Underwood will perform “America the Beautiful” at Donald Trump's inauguration next week and the 1970s hitmakers Village People will perform at two inaugural events.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: David Weiss condemns pardon, defends record in Hunter Biden prosecution report
item.description: Special Counsel David Weiss defended his handling of the Hunter Biden investigation in a final report capping his multi-year investigation into the president’s son.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis calls special session to implement Trump's immigration policies
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is calling state lawmakers into a special session to help carry out President-elect Donald Trump's promises for a swift crackdown on immigration.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First hearing in 'Trump-proof' California special session canceled as chairman's district hit by wildfires
item.description: A California Assembly committee chairman canceled the planned first hearing in a special "Trump-proof" session due to wildfires ravaging his legislative district.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A tough-on-crime approach is back in US state capitols
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Within minutes of his inauguration Monday, new Missouri Gov. Mike Kehoe unleashed a volley of orders targeting crime. The tone-setting move reflects a national trend. After a period of relaxed sentencing laws, a tough-on-crime approach is back in political favor in the U.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats eye effort to secure Jack Smith's Trump Jan. 6 report
item.description: The top Democrat on the Senate Judiciary Committee is asking the Justice Department to retain all records relating to special counsel Jack Smith, including his final report which has been tied up in litigation. Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cap-and-trade returns: NY plans to force big oil to ‘invest’ in ‘green’ by paying for emissions
item.description: In her State of the State address, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul is expected to propose a cap-and-invest program for energy producers that emit pollution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces student debt relief for 150,000 borrowers, bringing total helped to more than 5M
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it was forgiving student loans for more than 150,000 borrowers, bringing the total number of individuals impacted by debt relief in President Biden’s term to over 5 million.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Cannon allows for release of Smith report on Trump election interference
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon on Monday allowed the release of the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s efforts to block the peaceful transfer of power after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Here's when Trump nominees will appear for confirmation hearings
item.description: More than a dozen people nominated by President-elect Trump to serve in his administration will make their case to Senate committees this week. The busy week will kick off with the Senate Armed Services Committee hearing from Pete Hegseth on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Key Republican says he will support Gabbard as Trump DNI pick
item.description: Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., said in a Sunday interview that he will support President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Melania Trump planning to expand Be Best campaign in return as first lady
item.description: Melania Trump says she's aiming to expand her children's wellness platform, Be Best, when she returns to the White House as first lady. "I will continue with Be Best and also I will expand Be Best," Trump said in a Monday interview on "Fox & Friends.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom concerned Trump will try to withhold California fire aid
item.description: “He’s done it in Utah. He’s done it in Michigan, did it in Puerto Rico. He did it to California back before I was even governor, in 2018, until he found out folks in Orange County voted for him and he decided to give them money.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5

item.title: Bessent to divest from assets on road to leading Treasury
item.description: President-elect Trump’s Treasury Secretary pick Scott Bessent has plans to divest from funds and other investments.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Arizona's Democratic governor faces uphill battle as Republicans keep tight grip on legislature
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs knows she is outnumbered this legislative session, with Republicans having expanded their majorities in the statehouse after the last election.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CA firefighters face impersonators, staffing shortages, low pay
item.description: LA County Sheriff Robert Luna said he encountered a man dressed as a firefighter burglarizing a home.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Rahm Emanuel steps down as Japan ambassador ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: “Last year, I spoke at the U.S. Naval War College and found it both rewarding and reaffirming,” Emanuel said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans urge Trump nominees: Don't take the bait from Democrats
item.description: Senate Republicans are urging President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees to resist taking the bait from Democrats when their high-stakes confirmation hearings kick off next week, marking a make-or-break moment for some of his choices.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state by inserting Bible references into reading lessons and requiring teachers to post the Ten Commandments.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Hochul defends congestion pricing, explains inflation rebate checks
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In a few days, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul will deliver her State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Biden takes unexpected, impromptu questions from press in final days
item.description: President Biden opted Friday to voluntarily take a host of questions from the press after delivering remarks on the day’s job report, a speech that had been put on the White House schedule last minute.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada's Cortez Masto to reintroduce bill to fund fight against wildfires
item.description: In the wake of the devastating California wildfires, Nevada Democratic Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto plans to reintroduce a proposal in Congress to better fund fire departments, interagency coordination and recovery efforts.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: President-elect Trump appears virtually in court to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is appearing virtually in a New York courtroom to be sentenced for his hush money conviction, 10 days before he is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-FBI informant who fabricated bribery story about Biden and his son Hunter gets 6 years in prison
item.description: A former FBI informant who fabricated a story about President Joe Biden and his son Hunter accepting bribes that became central to Republicans’ impeachment effort was sentenced Wednesday to six years in prison.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: California governor proposes $322B budget with no deficit after two years of shortfalls
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has proposed a $322 billion budget without a deficit, a welcome change after two years of significant budget shortfalls in the nation's most populous state.
item.dc:creator: Tran Nguyen, Associated Press, Fox 5
